summary: Iteration 42 - Storage migration validation (local disk -> Emergent Object Storage). Backend pytest suite (16 tests) and frontend regression all PASS. New uploads (POST /api/upload, /api/attachments, /api/ativos/{id}/manual) correctly route to cloud and return file_url starting with /api/storage/. Proxy endpoint GET /api/storage/{path} serves files with correct Content-Type (image/jpeg, application/pdf) and returns 404 for unknown paths. Verified globally across 30 OS and 47 ativos: 0 attachments and 0 manuais still pointing to /api/uploads/ — full migration confirmed. Regression: Login (admin + tecnico), Dashboard (/api/dashboard/stats), OS Kanban (/api/ordens-servico), Ativos, Inspeções all return 200 and render correctly in the UI with prior testids intact. Logout works.

issues: Recharts 'width(-1)/height(-1)' warnings on initial render — pre-existing cosmetic issue documented in iteration 41; charts render correctly after layout settles.

critical_code_review_comments: storage.py:62 - global declaration ordering: `storage_key = None; key = init_storage()` uses module-level `storage_key` before global declaration in same function on retry path inside put_object/get_object. Works because storage_key is module-level, but consider explicit global hoisting at function top for clarity. Not a bug.

server.py:319 - GET /api/storage/{path:path} is unauthenticated. Files are reachable by anyone with the URL (UUID-based path provides obscurity but not authorization). If file confidentiality matters (manuals/anomalies may contain sensitive info), add auth dependency or signed URLs. Acceptable for current scope since URLs are random UUIDs but should be documented as a risk.

server.py:307-317 - Legacy /api/uploads/{filename} and /api/uploads/manuals/{filename} routes still exist. Since DB no longer references them (verified by tests), they're dead code unless used by external tools. Consider removing or keeping as documented fallback.

server.py:2266 - Local fallback file_url uses /api/uploads/{filename} but Manual fallback at 1908 uses /api/uploads/manuals/{filename}. Asymmetry is OK but worth a comment so future devs don't confuse paths.

storage.py is well-isolated and uses requests (sync) inside async FastAPI handlers. For large files this can block the event loop. Consider httpx.AsyncClient or running put_object in a threadpool (anyio.to_thread.run_sync) for better concurrency.
seed_data_creation: Test uploads tagged with TEST_*.jpg/TEST_manual.pdf prefix. No cleanup endpoint invoked — small number of test uploads (~7 files) acceptable. Manual cleanup possible via attachments DELETE endpoint if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Storage migration is fully validated. New uploads go to Emergent Object Storage via storage.py; URLs are /api/storage/manutrix/{entity}/{id}/{8hex}.{ext}. Proxy endpoint reads from cloud and 404s otherwise. Legacy /api/uploads/* routes still exist but DB no longer references them. To re-test, run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration42_storage.py -v`. Endpoints in app: /api/ordens-servico (NOT /work-orders), /api/dashboard/stats (NOT /dashboard).
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. Migration script populated MongoDB correctly and all upload endpoints/proxy work end-to-end.
